description: pypsrp is a pure-Python client for the PowerShell Remoting Protocol (PSRP). It allows you to invoke PowerShell commands on a remote Windows host.

documentation.example: from pypsrp.client import Client; c = Client('10.0.0.5', 'u', 'p'); c.execute_cmd('Get-Process')
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/pypsrp && python3 pypsrp.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/pypsrp && python3 pypsrp.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/pypsrp && python3 pypsrp.py --help`. Pure-Python PowerShell Remoting Protocol client.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the post_exploitation category. Fits when the engagement needs: Pure-Python PowerShell Remoting Protocol client.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Pure-Python PowerShell Remoting Protocol client.

metadata.example: from pypsrp.client import Client; c = Client('10.0.0.5', 'u', 'p'); c.execute_cmd('Get-Process')

name: pypsrp

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.
